message:
test(server/mail_ui): cover attachment rendering when agent rows are missing

New regression test for the mail_ui attachment render path
under the orphaned-agent-row scenario.  The earlier
"tolerate orphaned project metadata + harden mail status /
projects adopt / TUI compose paths" commit introduced
synthetic-agent-id fallbacks across multiple surfaces; this
test pins the mail_ui attachment render specifically.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/mail_ui.rs

- New render_attachments_preserves_messages_when_agent_rows_are_missing
  test:
    1. Build a fresh test pool with a unique project human_key.
    2. Register two agents (GreenCastle sender, BlueLake
       recipient).
    3. Create a message with a well-formed attachment.
    4. DELETE both agent rows from the agents table (leaving
       the message and its attachment_metadata intact).
    5. Call render_attachments(&pool, message_id) and assert:
        - The returned HTML contains the message subject.
        - The attachment filename is still rendered.
        - Both `[unknown-agent-<id>]` placeholders appear in
          the "From"/"To" cells in the rendered HTML instead
          of crashing, empty cells, or JSON fields bleeding
          through.
- Uses the existing test helpers (make_test_pool,
  unique_nonce, outcome_ok, block_on) from the same test
  module so the fixture scaffolding stays consistent with
  the neighboring tests.

No production code change — this is a test-only addition
that pins the existing fallback behavior so future changes
to the agent resolution path can't silently regress it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
